what_it_does: Implements the operator-gated write-back step of the registry-generation chain: it takes a confirmed batch of dossier entries, merges them additively into addresses.json, stamps data-ui-ref into the corresponding mockup HTML elements, and re-runs the canonical parser to regenerate element-map.json as a round-trip verification.
observations: Public entry point is registry_writeback, intended to be called only after human approval, never auto-run.
Batch is all-or-nothing: merge and stamp are computed in memory first; only if the whole batch validates are files written.
Uses a custom WritebackError that carries the offending address and refuses ambiguous/conflicting cases rather than guessing.
Merges registry entries additively and skips duplicates, but fails loudly on content conflicts with existing addresses.
Flattens structured howto into the canonical string format expected by consumers.
Derives region from the address via parse_ui_address and attaches provenance (run, model, confidence).
Stamps HTML with a two-tier exact-substring locate: full outerHTML first, then opening tag only; both require exactly one match.
Reuses parse.py:build_map for the round-trip re-parse instead of shelling or reimplementing.
Paths are parameters with real-corpus defaults so tests can point at temp copies.
Supports a dry-run mode (write=False) and a skip_already_stamped flag that records but does not refuse certain stamp conflicts.
standouts: The two-tier exact-substring stamping strategy with explicit rationale for leaf collisions and whitespace containers, plus a documented residual fail-loud rate.
The all-or-nothing in-memory validation before any disk mutation.
The skip_already_stamped additive path that keeps registry growth moving while recording stamp conflicts for a later selector-hardening pass.
conventions: Fail-loud: every ambiguity raises WritebackError rather than guessing.
Operator-only: the function is not wired to auto-run and expects an approval gate.
No silent overwrites: existing registry entries with different content are refused.
Parameterized paths with real defaults for safe testing.
Reuse over reimplementation: imports parse_ui_address and parse.py:build_map.
Provenance tracking for introspective data lineage.
Defensive deep-copy of the registry before mutation.
concerns: skip_already_stamped groups AMBIGUOUS locate failures with already-stamped skips, which could hide genuine locate problems.
_importable_as_pkg catches all exceptions silently, potentially masking import misconfigurations.
_stamp_html depends on exact byte-level substring matching; whitespace normalization or HTML minification could break tier-1.
There is no atomic write across mockups, addresses.json, and element-map.json; a crash after mockup writes but before addresses.json would leave files inconsistent.
The dry-run reparse maps buffers by basename only, which assumes unique filenames in mockups_dir.
notes: This is the guarded bridge that turns an approved registry proposal into canonical data and stamped mockups, then proves the join by re-parsing.
questions: What exact schema does parse.py:build_map return, and does it expose summary and elements keys as assumed?
How does parse_ui_address validate grammar and what does it return for malformed addresses?
Is skip_already_stamped intended for routine use or only a temporary selector-hardening path?
How should duplicate addresses within the same incoming batch be handled?
What is the expected behavior if the mockup file exists but the outerHTML is stale due to upstream edits?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose: it performs the merge/stamp/reparse round-trip under operator control and fail-loud semantics.
apparent_themes: One-truth registry with additive, non-destructive growth
Safe-by-construction exact matching for HTML stamping
intention_signals: The file is trying to make registry growth as safe and verifiable as a human-approved git operation: validate everything first, mutate only after approval, and immediately re-parse to confirm the join.
novelty: The two-tier exact-substring stamping heuristic with explicit recovery-rate commentary and a deliberate residual fail-loud bucket for genuinely ambiguous elements.
contribution: A concrete, fail-loud, all-or-nothing write-back operator that merges approved registry entries, stamps mockup HTML, and re-parses to verify the round-trip, with parameterized paths so it can be exercised safely against temp copies.
summary_for_embedding: registry_writeback.py is the operator-gated, fail-loud write-back function that merges approved UI registry entries into addresses.json, stamps data-ui-ref into mockup HTML, and re-parses to regenerate element-map.json as a round-trip verification.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to close the registry-generation loop by applying human-approved dossier entries to the canonical registry and mockups while refusing to guess or silently overwrite.

symbols.does: Custom exception class for fail-loud write-back refusals.
symbols.description: WritebackError extends Exception and carries an optional address field so callers can surface which registry entry caused the refusal.

symbols.does: Initializes the exception with a message and optional address.
symbols.description: Stores the human-readable refusal message and the offending ui:// address for upstream reporting.

symbols.does: Converts structured or string howto into the canonical flat string.
symbols.description: Accepts a dict with what/what_you_can_do/how_to_change keys or an already-flat string, and returns the WHAT / WHAT YOU CAN DO / HOW TO CHANGE IT format used by addresses.json.

symbols.does: Projects one confirmed dossier into an addresses.json-shaped record.
symbols.description: Validates the address grammar via parse_ui_address, derives region from the first segment, flattens howto, copies capabilities/represents/maps_to_feature, and nests provenance; it never fabricates a code reference.

symbols.does: Additively merges one record into the registry address map.
symbols.description: Adds new addresses, skips identical existing ones, and raises WritebackError when an existing address has different meaningful content, refusing silent overwrites.

symbols.does: Inserts data-ui-ref into an HTML opening tag.
symbols.description: Places the attribute immediately before the closing > or /> of the supplied opening tag, leaving children and text untouched.

symbols.does: Stamps data-ui-ref into the element identified by outerHTML in a mockup.
symbols.description: Uses a two-tier exact-substring locate (full outerHTML, then opening tag) requiring exactly one match; fails loudly if the element is missing, ambiguous, or already stamped.

symbols.does: Regenerates the element map by calling parse.py:build_map.
symbols.description: Reads every .html file in the mockups directory and invokes the canonical pure-function parser against the grown registry.

symbols.does: Checks whether design._system.parse is importable as a package.
symbols.description: Attempts a package import and returns True on success, swallowing any exception and returning False otherwise.

symbols.does: Fallback loader for parse.py by filesystem path.
symbols.description: Uses importlib.util to load parse.py from the same directory when it cannot be imported as a package.

symbols.does: Public operator-gated entry point for the full write-back round-trip.
symbols.description: Validates and merges entries, stamps mockups in memory, writes only after the whole batch succeeds, re-parses to verify the join, and returns a detailed report; supports dry-run and skip_already_stamped modes.

symbols.does: Re-parses for a dry run using in-memory stamped buffers.
symbols.description: Combines on-disk mockups for unstamped files with the in-memory stamped buffers for stamped files so the dry-run element-map join is accurate without writing to disk.
